ddls:
CREATE TABLE "packets" (
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet */,
  "offset" INTEGER /* Offset of the packet in the transmission buffer */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the packet in bytes */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of data in the packet (e.g., uint8_t, uint16_t, uint32_t) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the packet's purpose */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the packet's data */,
  "packet_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet type (e.g., data packet, control packet) */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of packet transmission or reception in milliseconds */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device that transmitted or received the packet */,
  "checksum" TEXT /* Checksum to ensure data integrity */,
  "retransmission_count" INTEGER /* Number of retransmissions if the packet fails */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the packet (e.g., transmitted, received, failed) */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source device */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the destination device */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the packet */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Error code encountered during transmission */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Detailed error message for debugging */,
  "compression_method" TEXT /* Compression method used, if any */,
  "encryption_method" TEXT /* Encryption method used, if any */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_packet_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_type_id") REFERENCES packet_types ("packet_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_types" (
  "packet_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet type */,
  "packet_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the packet type (e.g., data packet, control packet) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the packet type's purpose */,
  "data_structure" TEXT /* Structure of the packet data (e.g., JSON schema) */,
  "max_size" INTEGER /* Maximum size of the packet in bytes */,
  "min_size" INTEGER /* Minimum size of the packet in bytes */,
  "default_retransmission_limit" INTEGER /* Default number of retransmissions allowed for this packet type */,
  "default_timeout" INTEGER /* Default timeout for this packet type in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., IoT sensor, gateway) */,
  "device_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the device */,
  "last_seen" INTEGER /* Last time the device was seen */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the device (e.g., online, offline, maintenance) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for the device */,
  "protocol_version" TEXT /* Version of the communication protocol used by the device */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Version of the firmware running on the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, technician) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "created_at" INTEGER /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource the permission applies to (e.g., packets, devices) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action allowed (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "scope" TEXT /* Scope of the permission (e.g., all, specific device) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "apis" (
  "api_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API */,
  "api_name" TEXT /* Name of the API */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the API */,
  "endpoint" TEXT /* Endpoint URL of the API */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) */,
  "authentication_required" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if authentication is required */,
  "created_at" INTEGER /* Timestamp of when the API was created */,
  "last_updated" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the last update to the API */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("api_id")
)
